2026-04-22T06:31:49.2018744Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T06:31:49.2019179Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T06:31:49.2019548Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T06:31:49.2019865Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T06:31:49.2020101Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-22T06:32:57.0825645Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T06:32:42.832875296Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58484,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] ParameterContextRegistry.FindParameter crashes when multiple parameters share the same WireInfo.SerializedName\",\"body\":\"### Bug Report: MPG Generator crashes in ParameterContextRegistry.FindParameter when multiple parameters share the same WireInfo.SerializedName\\n\\n**Generator Version**: `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` v1.0.0-alpha.20260419.1\\n\\n**Crash Location**: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Models/OperationContexts/ParameterContextRegistry.cs` line 149\\n\\n**Error**: `System.InvalidOperationException: Sequence contains more than one matching element` at `SingleOrDefault`\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Root Cause\\n\\n`ParameterContextRegistry.FindParameter` at line 149 uses:\\n```csharp\\nSingleOrDefault(p =\\u0026gt; p.WireInfo.SerializedName == parameterToFind.WireInfo.SerializedName)\\n```\\nThis crashes when two or more parameters in `methodParameters` share the same `WireInfo.SerializedName`. Two distinct scenarios trigger this:\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Scenario 1: Duplicate `@segment(\\u0026#34;providers\\u0026#34;)` path parameters\\n\\n**Spec pattern** (`routes.tsp`):\\n```typespec\\n@autoRoute @get @action(\\u0026#34;balances\\u0026#34;)\\ngetByBillingAccount(\\n    @path @segment(\\u0026#34;providers\\u0026#34;) @key providerNamespace: \\u0026#34;Microsoft.Billing\\u0026#34;,\\n    @path @segment(\\u0026#34;billingAccounts\\u0026#34;) billingAccountId: string,\\n    ...Azure.ResourceManager.Legacy.Provider,\\n): Balance | ErrorResponse;\\n```\\n\\n**Problem**: The explicit `@segment(\\u0026#34;providers\\u0026#34;)` on `providerNamespace` and the spread `...Azure.ResourceManager.Legacy.Provider` (which adds another `@path @segment(\\u0026#34;providers\\u0026#34;) provider`) both produce path parameters with `WireInfo.SerializedName = \\u0026#34;providers\\u0026#34;`. When `FindParameter` searches by serialized name, `SingleOrDefault` finds 2 matches and throws.\\n\\n**Affected operations**: Any operation that references an external resource provider (e.g., Microsoft.Billing, Microsoft.Capacity, Microsoft.Management) via `@segment(\\u0026#34;providers\\u0026#34;)` while also using `...Legacy.Provider` for Microsoft.Consumption. In the Consumption spec, **16 operations** are affected.\\n\\n**Workaround**: Replace `@autoRoute + @action + @segment + ...Legacy.Provider` with an explicit `@route(...)` path. However, this changes the generated swagger/openapi.json output, which may not be acceptable for spec compliance.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Scenario 2: `scope` query parameter name conflicts with auto-created `scopeParameter`\\n\\n**Spec pattern** (`routes.tsp`):\\n```typespec\\n@autoRoute @get @action(\\u0026#34;reservationRecommendationDetails\\u0026#34;)\\nget(\\n    @path(#{ allowReserved: true }) @segment(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;) resourceScope: string,\\n    ...Azure.ResourceManager.Legacy.Provider,\\n    @query(\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;) scope: Scope,\\n    ...\\n): ReservationRecommendationDetailsModel;\\n```\\n\\n**Problem**: `MockableArmClientProvider.BuildMethods()` (line 67) creates a `scopeParameter = new ParameterProvider(\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;, ...)` for ALL extension-scoped non-resource methods. The `ParameterProvider` constructor auto-generates `WireInfo` from the parameter name when none is explicitly provided, so `new ParameterProvider(\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;, ...)` produces `WireInfo.SerializedName = \\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;`. The TypeSpec query parameter `@query(\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;)` also produces `WireInfo.SerializedName = \\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;`. Both end up in `methodParameters`, causing `SingleOrDefault` to find 2 matches.\\n\\n**Key detail**: Simply renaming the TypeSpec parameter name (e.g., `reservationScope`) while keeping `@query(\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;)` does NOT fix the issue, because `WireInfo.SerializedName` comes from the `@query` decorator value, not the parameter name.\\n\\n**Workaround**: Change `@query(\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;)` to `@query(\\u0026#34;reservationScope\\u0026#34;)` in TypeSpec. But this changes the HTTP wire format from `?scope=Single` to `?reservationScope=Single`, which breaks API compatibility with the actual Azure service endpoint.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Suggested Fix\\n\\nIn `ParameterContextRegistry.FindParameter`, consider:\\n1. Using `FirstOrDefault` instead of `SingleOrDefault` when a unique match isn\\u0026#39;t guaranteed\\n2. Adding a secondary discriminator (e.g., parameter location: path vs query vs header) when multiple parameters share the same serialized name\\n3. For the scope conflict specifically: the auto-created `scopeParameter` in `MockableArmClientProvider` should use a distinct `WireInfo.SerializedName` (or skip WireInfo entirely since it\\u0026#39;s a synthetic ResourceIdentifier parameter, not a wire-transmitted parameter)\\n\\n### Reproduction\\n\\nAny TypeSpec spec with these patterns will crash the C# MPG generator. The `Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption` spec (api-version 2024-08-01) reproduces both scenarios.\\n\\n### Impact\\n\\n- **Consumption SDK migration** is blocked from clean regeneration\\n- Any future spec using `@segment(\\u0026#34;providers\\u0026#34;)` with `...Legacy.Provider`, or having a `@query(\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;)` on extension-scoped operations, will hit the same crash\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58484\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"mzhongl524\",\"id\":13110707,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/mzhongl524\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13110707?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-22T06:30:23Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-22T06:30:23Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-22T06:32:57.0867963Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T06:32:43.100129811Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58484,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] ParameterContextRegistry.FindParameter crashes when multiple parameters share the same WireInfo.SerializedName\",\"body\":\"### Bug Report: MPG Generator crashes in ParameterContextRegistry.FindParameter when multiple parameters share the same WireInfo.SerializedName\\n\\n**Generator Version**: `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` v1.0.0-alpha.20260419.1\\n\\n**Crash Location**: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Models/OperationContexts/ParameterContextRegistry.cs` line 149\\n\\n**Error**: `System.InvalidOperationException: Sequence contains more than one matching element` at `SingleOrDefault`\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Root Cause\\n\\n`ParameterContextRegistry.FindParameter` at line 149 uses:\\n```csharp\\nSingleOrDefault(p =\\u0026gt; p.WireInfo.SerializedName == parameterToFind.WireInfo.SerializedName)\\n```\\nThis crashes when two or more parameters in `methodParameters` share the same `WireInfo.SerializedName`. Two distinct scenarios trigger this:\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Scenario 1: Duplicate `@segment(\\u0026#34;providers\\u0026#34;)` path parameters\\n\\n**Spec pattern** (`routes.tsp`):\\n```typespec\\n@autoRoute @get @action(\\u0026#34;balances\\u0026#34;)\\ngetByBillingAccount(\\n    @path @segment(\\u0026#34;providers\\u0026#34;) @key providerNamespace: \\u0026#34;Microsoft.Billing\\u0026#34;,\\n    @path @segment(\\u0026#34;billingAccounts\\u0026#34;) billingAccountId: string,\\n    ...Azure.ResourceManager.Legacy.Provider,\\n): Balance | ErrorResponse;\\n```\\n\\n**Problem**: The explicit `@segment(\\u0026#34;providers\\u0026#34;)` on `providerNamespace` and the spread `...Azure.ResourceManager.Legacy.Provider` (which adds another `@path @segment(\\u0026#34;providers\\u0026#34;) provider`) both produce path parameters with `WireInfo.SerializedName = \\u0026#34;providers\\u0026#34;`. When `FindParameter` searches by serialized name, `SingleOrDefault` finds 2 matches and throws.\\n\\n**Affected operations**: Any operation that references an external resource provider (e.g., Microsoft.Billing, Microsoft.Capacity, Microsoft.Management) via `@segment(\\u0026#34;providers\\u0026#34;)` while also using `...Legacy.Provider` for Microsoft.Consumption. In the Consumption spec, **16 operations** are affected.\\n\\n**Workaround**: Replace `@autoRoute + @action + @segment + ...Legacy.Provider` with an explicit `@route(...)` path. However, this changes the generated swagger/openapi.json output, which may not be acceptable for spec compliance.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Scenario 2: `scope` query parameter name conflicts with auto-created `scopeParameter`\\n\\n**Spec pattern** (`routes.tsp`):\\n```typespec\\n@autoRoute @get @action(\\u0026#34;reservationRecommendationDetails\\u0026#34;)\\nget(\\n    @path(#{ allowReserved: true }) @segment(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;) resourceScope: string,\\n    ...Azure.ResourceManager.Legacy.Provider,\\n    @query(\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;) scope: Scope,\\n    ...\\n): ReservationRecommendationDetailsModel;\\n```\\n\\n**Problem**: `MockableArmClientProvider.BuildMethods()` (line 67) creates a `scopeParameter = new ParameterProvider(\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;, ...)` for ALL extension-scoped non-resource methods. The `ParameterProvider` constructor auto-generates `WireInfo` from the parameter name when none is explicitly provided, so `new ParameterProvider(\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;, ...)` produces `WireInfo.SerializedName = \\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;`. The TypeSpec query parameter `@query(\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;)` also produces `WireInfo.SerializedName = \\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;`. Both end up in `methodParameters`, causing `SingleOrDefault` to find 2 matches.\\n\\n**Key detail**: Simply renaming the TypeSpec parameter name (e.g., `reservationScope`) while keeping `@query(\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;)` does NOT fix the issue, because `WireInfo.SerializedName` comes from the `@query` decorator value, not the parameter name.\\n\\n**Workaround**: Change `@query(\\u0026#34;scope\\u0026#34;)` to `@query(\\u0026#34;reservationScope\\u0026#34;)` in TypeSpec. But this changes the HTTP wire format from `?scope=Single` to `?reservationScope=Single`, which breaks API compatibility with the actual Azure service endpoint.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Suggested Fix\\n\\nIn `ParameterContextRegistry.FindParameter`, consider:\\n1. Using `FirstOrDefault` instead of `SingleOrDefault` when a unique match isn\\u0026#39;t guaranteed\\n2. Adding a secondary discriminator (e.g., parameter location: path vs query vs header) when multiple parameters share the same serialized name\\n3.
